description: Deterministic validation contract for NCC PDF ingestion and XML-linked validation with strategy-driven extraction and candidate-backed semantic promotion.

representation_model.alignment_dependency: PDF-to-XML alignment can only support candidate extraction and later semantic progression when the paired XML source has passed its gate and alignment evidence remains within configured thresholds.
representation_model.strategy_routing: Extraction may be routed by document strategy so that different NCC section families can use different extractor modes while remaining bound to the same validation contract.

operational_assumptions.mode_selection_rule: Known table-heavy PDFs may enable Docling table structure when benchmark evidence shows materially better table recovery.
operational_assumptions.contract_invariance: Changing extractor mode must not relax structure, alignment, metadata, or quality requirements.
operational_assumptions.table_linking_current_state: Extracted PDF tables may be emitted without XML linkage fields when no deterministic XML-side table mapping exists yet.
operational_assumptions.current_gate_semantics: The existing schema field can_progress_to_semantic_layer is retained for compatibility, but in the staged architecture it should be interpreted as permission to enter candidate extraction, not direct canonical snippet creation.
operational_assumptions.document_family_id_policy: The paired PDF/XML family identifier must stay deterministic, but may be shortened with a stable hash suffix when required to satisfy persistence limits.

status_model.PASS: All blocking rules pass and no warning outcomes remain.
status_model.PASS_WITH_WARNINGS: All blocking rules pass and only bounded warning outcomes remain.
status_model.REVIEW_REQUIRED: The PDF output is structurally usable but requires review before candidate extraction and semantic progression.
status_model.FAIL: Validation completed and the PDF output did not satisfy contract requirements.
status_model.BLOCKED: A blocking rule prevents progression to the candidate and semantic layers.

rules.description: The PDF must be text-based and parseable enough to support deterministic extraction.
The PDF must be split to the required section-level scope before canonical ingestion proceeds.
Every extracted fragment must retain a bounding box, type, page reference, and stable fragment identity.
rules.pass_when: fragments.total > 0 AND fragments.missing_bbox <= thresholds.max_missing_bbox AND fragments.untyped == thresholds.max_untyped_fragments AND fragments.missing_page_reference == 0 AND fragments.missing_fragment_id == 0
rules.block_when: fragments.total == 0 OR fragments.missing_bbox > thresholds.max_missing_bbox OR fragments.untyped > thresholds.max_untyped_fragments OR fragments.missing_page_reference > 0 OR fragments.missing_fragment_id > 0

rules.description: Extracted PDF tables must preserve row structure and usable header information where present, regardless of which extractor mode produced them.
rules.pass_when: tables.invalid_count <= thresholds.max_invalid_tables AND tables.empty_row_sets == 0 AND tables.missing_headers_where_expected == 0
rules.block_when: tables.invalid_count > thresholds.max_invalid_tables OR tables.empty_row_sets > 0 OR tables.missing_headers_where_expected > 0

rules.description: PDF fragments must align to the paired XML representation with sufficient confidence and bounded unresolved mappings.
rules.pass_when: xml_validation.can_progress_to_alignment_layer == true AND alignment.unresolved == 0 AND alignment.low_confidence == 0 AND alignment.average_confidence >= thresholds.preferred_alignment_confidence
rules.warn_when: xml_validation.can_progress_to_alignment_layer == true AND alignment.average_confidence >= thresholds.min_alignment_confidence_for_warning AND alignment.unresolved <= thresholds.max_unresolved_alignments_for_warning AND alignment.low_confidence <= thresholds.max_low_confidence_alignments_for_warning
rules.block_when: xml_validation.can_progress_to_alignment_layer != true OR alignment.average_confidence < thresholds.min_alignment_confidence_for_warning OR alignment.unresolved > thresholds.max_unresolved_alignments_for_warning OR alignment.low_confidence > thresholds.max_low_confidence_alignments_for_warning

rules.description: Fragments must carry sufficient metadata to support clause traceability, page provenance, and XML relationship lookup.
Overall PDF ingestion quality score must meet the configured threshold before candidate extraction and later semantic progression.

table_validation_contract.current_behavior: XML linkage fields are only emitted when a concrete XML-side table mapping exists.
future_direction: explicit linked or unlinked or ambiguous mapping states
XML bundle support for one PDF to many XML sources
lineage metadata that distinguishes extracted table identity from XML table identity

companion_strategy_metadata.description: Strategy metadata may be emitted alongside the validated result to explain which extractor configuration was selected.

review_workspace_payload.description: The ingestion response may expose a transitional lineage payload to support UI review workspaces before the first-class candidate runtime is fully implemented.

classification_model.xml_structural_class: Primary semantic typing signal when a linked XML node exists.
classification_model.pdf_evidence_class: Rendered evidence class retained as first-class review metadata even when semantic typing remains XML-led.
classification_model.candidate_semantic_class: Reconciled candidate semantic class for review, workflow, and future graph queries.
classification_model.review_issue_class: Stable triage facet describing the dominant mismatch or review issue type for a surfaced candidate.
classification_model.review_source_emphasis: Stable triage facet describing whether the current issue is XML-driven, PDF-driven, mixed, or balanced.
classification_model.needs_human_review: Boolean triage facet indicating whether the candidate belongs in the operator review subset.
classification_model.candidate_type_compatibility: candidate_type may be retained as a compatibility alias for candidate_semantic_class.
classification_model.mismatch_policy_note: Raw token deltas may be preserved separately from effective mismatch deltas so structural title tokens do not force rule-style mismatches.

count_model.candidate_total: Total candidates created by the validation pass before any review workspace narrowing.
count_model.candidate_surfaced: Candidates included in the current review workspace after any focused narrowing.
count_model.candidate_needs_review: Subset of surfaced candidates still requiring human review.

ui_behavior_notes: the UI may show in-flight validation progress while awaiting the response
temporary progress feedback is not a validation-status outcome
storage-safe document family identifiers must remain deterministic for the same PDF/XML pair
retained runs may be reloaded through an explicit run-detail API such as GET /api/ingestions/runs/{run_id}
reloaded run payloads should restore validation outputs, lineage, review workspace state, and persisted reviewer decisions without recomputing extraction at request time
a retained PDF file endpoint such as GET /api/ingestions/runs/{run_id}/pdf may still exist, but the UI is not required to auto-embed that file after refresh
the current review console may require an explicit Relink PDF action so the operator can reattach the local PDF in the current browser session
when the operator relinks the PDF locally, the embedded preview should preserve page-level candidate navigation even when exact in-page highlighting is not yet implemented
the review sidebar may support filter tabs, explicit sort modes such as confidence, issue class, and XML/PDF source emphasis, plus pagination for larger surfaced candidate sets

candidate_stage_contract.entry_requirement: Validated PDF and XML alignment evidence may only authorize candidate extraction, not direct canonical snippet creation.
candidate_stage_contract.snippet_promotion_rule: Canonical snippets must originate from validated candidates.
future_schema_direction: candidate promotion evidence in validation or lineage payloads
